lesson_contract.objective: Learn the BMH Group service standard and the mindset that keeps seller conversations clear, calm, and human.
objective_points: Use curiosity and clarity in seller conversations
Detach from outcomes while staying accountable
lesson_contract.transition_spoken_text: Take the Welcome and Mindset checkpoint next. Then move into Terms Glossary.

forbidden_language_removals.replacement_rule: Use decision-stage language with no fixed seller progression.
Use decision-stage language with no fixed seller progression.

scenes.title: How you enter the conversation
scenes.spoken_text: Before you start a conversation, settle your attention. You are there to understand the situation, help the seller see the available next step, and keep the record accurate. You do not need to force an outcome.

visual_plan.shot: Andrea standing in a medium shot
visual_plan.editor_note: Open with a calm breath and grounded posture.

scenes.title: Service over self
scenes.spoken_text: Start with service. Assume the conversation should be useful even when the seller chooses another path. When you stop chasing the result, you can listen for what would actually help.

visual_plan.editor_note: Optional cutaway may show a seller sorting inherited-property paperwork.

scenes.title: Get on their side of the table
scenes.spoken_text: Try to see the situation from the seller's side. A property problem can also be a time problem, a family problem, or a stress problem. Name what you heard before you ask the next question.

visual_plan.editor_note: Use a listening posture and a small nod.

scenes.title: Clarity before pressure
scenes.spoken_text: When someone is unsure, help them make the decision clearer. Ask what a workable outcome would look like, what needs to happen first, and what information is missing. A visible path is easier to evaluate than a rushed decision.

visual_plan.shot: Andrea beside a simple two-path diagram
visual_plan.editor_note: Keep the diagram generic and free of promised outcomes.

scenes.title: Own your actions
scenes.spoken_text: You cannot control whether someone answers, agrees, or changes their mind. You can control your preparation, your tone, the quality of your notes, and whether you complete the next action you promised.

visual_plan.shot: Andrea in a closer medium frame
visual_plan.editor_note: Shift to a more direct posture for accountability.

scenes.title: Curiosity over cleverness
scenes.spoken_text: Simple questions work. Ask one, then give the person room to answer. If you are planning your next line while they are still talking, you are likely to miss the useful detail.

visual_plan.editor_note: Hold a natural pause after the second sentence.

scenes.title: Use equal status
scenes.spoken_text: Speak peer to peer. Do not beg for agreement, and do not lecture. If something does not fit, say so respectfully. Direct language and a steady tone make the conversation easier to trust.

scenes.title: Let decisions mature
scenes.spoken_text: A seller who is not ready now may become ready after the situation changes or the options become clearer. Follow up with a useful reason. Bring new information when you have it. Let the seller's decision stage guide the conversation instead of assuming a fixed timeline.

visual_plan.editor_note: Optional cutaway may show a neutral sequence of follow-up notes with no dates.

scenes.title: Treat problems as missing information
scenes.spoken_text: An objection is often a missing piece. If someone says they need more money, ask what the money needs to cover. If they need more time, ask what has to happen before they can move. The answer tells you whether there is a responsible next step.

visual_plan.shot: Andrea beside a small tabletop card
visual_plan.editor_note: Use one simple puzzle-piece prop if desired. No floating icon montage.

scenes.title: Practice creates calm
scenes.spoken_text: Calm comes from practice and review. Listen back when that is part of the current process. Notice where you rushed, filled silence, or missed a question. Apply one correction in the next conversation.

visual_plan.shot: Andrea at a desk reviewing notes
visual_plan.editor_note: Keep any review screen generic and unbranded.

scenes.title: Understand before you argue
scenes.spoken_text: If you feel yourself trying to win the point, slow down. Restate what the seller is concerned about and check that you have it right. Understanding creates more movement than an argument.

visual_plan.shot: Andrea in a centered medium shot
visual_plan.editor_note: Use a small reset gesture at the opening.

scenes.title: Continue to the checkpoint
scenes.spoken_text: Take the Welcome and Mindset checkpoint next. Then move into Terms Glossary.

visual_plan.shot: Andrea centered in a medium shot

edit_operations.source_problem: Fixed week progression is embedded in the long-game principle. A clean full narration keeps all ten principles while removing the timeline claim.
